pi_items.metadata_value: Expected a 'metadata' field in task.json containing claims
pi_items.verified_value: No 'metadata' field exists in task.json
pi_items.source: Direct inspection of task.json structure

pi_items.verified_value: 10mg is a standard starting dose for Lisinopril; typical range 10-40mg once daily
pi_items.source: Drugs.com Lisinopril Dosage Guide, Mayo Clinic

pi_items.verified_value: Lisinopril is typically taken once daily (24 hours), not 12 hours. Twice-daily dosing is non-standard and rarely used.
pi_items.source: Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, NHS - all specify once-daily dosing

pi_items.verified_value: 500mg is a standard dose; typical starting dosage is 500mg twice daily
pi_items.source: Drugs.com Metformin Dosage Guide, Mayo Clinic, NHS

pi_items.verified_value: Metformin is typically taken 2-3 times daily (roughly 8-12 hours apart). 12-hour dosing implies twice daily, which is within normal range.
pi_items.source: Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, NHS Metformin guidance

pi_items.metadata_value: Task requires changing Metformin frequency from 12 to 8 hours (from README.md)
pi_items.verified_value: 8-hour frequency implies 3 times daily (24÷8=3), which is within the normal Metformin dosing range of 2-3 times daily
pi_items.source: Mayo Clinic - Metformin can be taken 2-3 times daily

pi_items.verified_value: 20mg is a standard starting dose; typical range is 10-80mg once daily
pi_items.source: Drugs.com Atorvastatin Dosage Guide, Mayo Clinic

pi_items.verified_value: Atorvastatin is taken once daily (24 hours) - this is the standard and only dosing frequency
pi_items.source: Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, FDA labeling

pi_items.verified_value: 81mg is the standard 'baby aspirin' dose for daily use, within the typical range of 75-100mg
pi_items.source: GoodRx Aspirin Dosage, Mayo Clinic, FDA - Bayer Low Dose Aspirin

pi_items.verified_value: Low-dose aspirin (81mg) is typically taken once daily (24 hours) for cardiovascular protection
pi_items.source: GoodRx, Cleveland Clinic, FDA labeling

pi_items.metadata_value: Task requires updating Aspirin last dose to 8:00 AM today (from README.md line 36)
pi_items.verified_value: Requirement is logically sound - updating to a specific time within the day is a realistic task scenario
pi_items.source: Task description analysis

pi_items.verified_value: 300mg is a standard dose; typical dosing is 300mg three times daily (900mg/day total)
pi_items.source: Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, NHS Gabapentin guidance

pi_items.verified_value: Gabapentin maintenance dosing is 900-1800mg per day in 3 divided doses, which equals 8-hour intervals. This is correct.
pi_items.source: Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, NHS - standard 3x daily dosing

pi_items.metadata_value: LibreOffice Calc stores time as fractional days (from README.md lines 69-77)
pi_items.verified_value: Correct - Calc uses fractional day representation where 1.0 = 24 hours, 0.5 = 12 hours, etc.
pi_items.source: LibreOffice Calc official documentation - Calculating With Dates and Times

pi_items.metadata_value: Formula =D2+(C2/24) calculates next dose by adding hours divided by 24 (from README.md line 40)
pi_items.verified_value: Correct formula structure - dividing hours by 24 converts to fractional days for proper time arithmetic
pi_items.source: LibreOffice Calc documentation on time arithmetic

pi_items.metadata_value: Use formula E2<(NOW()+(2/24)) to highlight doses due within 2 hours (from README.md lines 49-50)
pi_items.verified_value: Correct approach - NOW() returns current time as fractional day, adding (2/24) represents +2 hours
pi_items.source: LibreOffice Calc documentation on TIMEVALUE and conditional formatting

pi_items.metadata_value: Data origin claim (implied synthetic from setup script)
pi_items.verified_value: Data is synthetically generated at task runtime using current system date via bash script
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 13-14 use $(date) and $(date -d 'yesterday')

pi_items.metadata_value: Medications list: Lisinopril, Metformin, Atorvastatin, Aspirin, Gabapentin (from README.md)
pi_items.verified_value: All medications are real pharmaceutical drugs; combination is realistic for eldercare/chronic disease management
pi_items.source: All verified through FDA/drug reference databases (Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, etc.)

privileged_info_summary: The medication schedule update task uses synthetically generated data created at runtime using current system date. Medications are all real pharmaceutical drugs with verified dosages: Lisinopril 10mg (standard), Metformin 500mg (standard), Atorvastatin 20mg (standard), Aspirin 81mg (standard baby aspirin), Gabapentin 300mg (standard). Medication frequencies are medically plausible with one exception: Lisinopril is specified as 12-hour frequency but is clinically taken once daily (24 hours). All other dosing frequencies (Metformin 12h, Atorvastatin 24h, Aspirin 24h, Gabapentin 8h) are verified against medical references. LibreOffice Calc time arithmetic methodology is correct: fractional day representation with formula =D2+(C2/24) for hour-to-day conversion. Conditional formatting using NOW()+(2/24) for 2-hour window detection is technically sound.
